source.title: The Friendship Bench Program — Interview with Grandma Moyo

sentences.text: That's really amazing!
Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda.
Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program.
Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program?
Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo.
I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years.
I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it.
I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them.
That's very kind of you.
Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program?
It's been wonderful.
Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of.
I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives.
One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away.
I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey.
Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business!
Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program.
It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives.
Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo.
It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had.
And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 주제로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives.
choices: a grandmother volunteer's personal experience and dedication in a community mental health program
the medical training process that elderly volunteers must complete before counseling patients
the economic impact of mental health services on small business development in Zimbabwe
the challenges faced by healthcare workers in providing therapy to young patients
the role of government agencies in recruiting volunteers for public health initiatives
problems.explanation: Grandma Moyo가 8년간 자원봉사하며 지역사회 정신건강 문제를 돕고, 한 환자의 회복 사례를 통해 보람을 느낀다는 개인 경험과 헌신이 핵심 주제이다. ②는 의학 훈련 과정, ③은 경제적 영향, ④는 의료진의 어려움, ⑤는 정부 기관의 모집 역할로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 요지로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: Community volunteers can make a meaningful impact on people's mental health through compassionate support.
Professional qualifications are more important than personal motivation in mental health counseling.
Young people are the only group that benefits from community-based mental health programs.
Mental health problems can only be solved through long-term medication and hospitalization.
Volunteering is primarily beneficial for the volunteers themselves rather than the people they help.
problems.explanation: Grandma Moyo가 자원봉사자로서 공감과 지지를 통해 환자들의 삶에 의미 있는 변화를 만들어냈다는 것이 요지이다. ①은 전문 자격이 더 중요하다는 반대 주장, ③은 젊은이만 혜택이라는 과도한 한정, ④는 약물·입원만 해결책이라는 주장, ⑤는 자원봉사가 봉사자에게만 유익하다는 오해이다.

problems.instruction: Grandma Moyo가 프로그램에 참여한 목적으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: to earn extra income by providing professional counseling services
to gain recognition as a leading healthcare professional in Zimbabwe
to help community members struggling with mental health problems
to fulfill a government requirement for community service hours
to learn new skills for her own personal career development
problems.explanation: ⑦번 문장에서 '지역사회 많은 사람들이 정신건강 문제로 어려움을 겪고 있어서 무언가 하고 싶었다'고 직접 밝혔다. ①은 수입, ③은 인정, ④는 정부 요구, ⑤는 자기 경력 개발로 본문의 동기와 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. <u>I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them.</u> That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: Grandma Moyo believed that emotional support could help people recover from psychological pain.
Grandma Moyo planned to provide physical medical treatment to patients with heart conditions.
Grandma Moyo intended to replace professional doctors in treating serious mental illnesses.
Grandma Moyo thought that only experienced therapists could truly heal emotional suffering.
Grandma Moyo was confident that a single conversation would permanently solve all mental health issues.
problems.explanation: '상처받은 마음을 치유하는 것을 도울 수 있다'는 정서적 지지를 통해 심리적 고통에서 회복하도록 도울 수 있다는 믿음을 의미한다. ①은 심장 질환 물리적 치료, ③은 전문 의사 대체, ④는 경험 많은 치료사만 가능, ⑤는 한 번의 대화로 해결이라는 극단적 해석이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 문장이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. <u>Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of.</u> I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: People should feel embarrassed about asking for mental health support.
There is no shame in reaching out for support when dealing with mental health difficulties.
Mental health issues are not real problems and do not require professional help.
Only those with severe mental illnesses should seek help from counseling programs.
Patients who seek help are weaker than those who handle problems on their own.
problems.explanation: '정신건강 문제에 대해 도움을 구하는 것은 부끄러워할 일이 아니다'는 정신건강 어려움을 겪을 때 지원을 구하는 것에 수치심이 없다는 의미이다. ①은 반대 의미, ②는 정신건강 문제가 실제가 아니라는 주장, ④는 심각한 경우만, ⑤는 도움을 구하는 사람이 약하다는 편견이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. <u>Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business!</u> Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: The girl's business was already established before she received counseling from Grandma Moyo.
The girl started a business specifically to help other people with mental health problems.
The counseling helped the girl recover emotionally and regain stability in her life.
Grandma Moyo provided the girl with financial support to start her business.
The girl no longer needs any form of emotional support after starting her business.
problems.explanation: 어머니를 잃고 어려운 시기를 겪던 소녀가 상담 후 '삶이 정상 궤도로 돌아오고 사업까지 시작했다'는 것은 상담이 정서적 회복과 삶의 안정을 되찾는 데 도움이 되었음을 보여준다. ①은 사업이 이미 있었다는 오해, ③은 정신건강 사업이라는 근거 없는 추론, ④는 재정 지원, ⑤는 더 이상 지원 불필요라는 과도한 추론이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분이 의미하는 바로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! <u>Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives.</u> Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: Grandma Moyo feels that her volunteer work is fulfilling because she witnesses real improvements in people's lives.
Grandma Moyo is proud mainly because the program has received international awards and recognition.
Grandma Moyo believes that only dramatic success stories justify the effort of volunteering.
Grandma Moyo considers volunteering rewarding because it provides her with a steady income.
Grandma Moyo thinks that positive changes happen automatically without any counseling effort.
problems.explanation: '이런 사례들이 프로그램의 일원인 것을 자랑스럽게 만든다'와 '사람들이 긍정적 변화를 만드는 것을 돕는 보람 있는 경험'이라는 표현은 사람들의 삶에서 실제 개선을 목격하기 때문에 자원봉사가 보람차다는 의미이다. ②는 국제상, ③은 극적 성공만 가치, ④는 수입, ⑤는 자동 변화라는 오해이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 부분으로부터 추론할 수 있는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. <u>It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.</u>
choices: The host believes the program has had no measurable impact on the community.
The success of the program is entirely due to government funding and policy changes.
The host suggests that Dr. Chibanda alone deserves all the credit for the program.
The program's influence has been limited to a small number of patients in one region.
The program's success is attributed to the dedicated efforts of its volunteers and founder.
problems.explanation: '프로그램이 끼친 영향을 보는 것이 정말 영감을 준다'와 '당신과 Dr. Chibanda 같은 사람들의 노력 덕분'이라는 것은 프로그램의 성공이 자원봉사자와 설립자의 헌신적 노력 덕분이라는 의미이다. ②는 영향 없음, ③은 정부 자금만, ④는 Dr. Chibanda만, ⑤는 소수 환자·한 지역으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community ________________ with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: comply
struggle
compete
experiment
disagree
problems.explanation: struggle with = '~로 어려움을 겪다'. 정신건강 문제로 어려움을 겪는다는 문맥에 적절하다. comply(순응), compete(경쟁), experiment(실험), disagree(동의하지 않다)는 문맥에 맞지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by ________________ them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: ignoring
criticizing
replacing
supporting
isolating
problems.explanation: '그들을 지지함으로써 상처받은 마음을 치유하는 것을 도울 수 있다'는 문맥에서 supporting(지지하는)이 적절하다. ignoring(무시), criticizing(비판), replacing(대체), isolating(격리)은 치유와 반대되는 행동이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ________________ of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: ashamed
proud
aware
afraid
capable
problems.explanation: nothing to be ashamed of = '부끄러워할 것이 없는'. 정신건강 도움을 구하는 것이 수치스럽지 않다는 맥락. proud(자랑스러운), aware(인식하는), afraid(두려운), capable(능력 있는)는 'nothing to be ~ of' 구문에서 원문과 다른 의미가 된다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom ________________. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: moved abroad
changed careers
started a business
passed away
retired early
problems.explanation: pass away = '돌아가시다'. 어머니가 돌아가신 후 어려운 시기를 겪고 있었다는 문맥. moved abroad(해외 이주), changed careers(전직), started a business(사업 시작), retired early(조기 퇴직)는 '어려운 시기'의 원인으로 부적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is ________________, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: falling apart rapidly
completely unchanged
dependent on medication
back on track
worse than before
problems.explanation: back on track = '정상 궤도로 돌아온'. 상담 후 삶이 회복되어 사업까지 시작했다는 긍정적 맥락. falling apart(무너지는), completely unchanged(완전히 변하지 않은), dependent on medication(약물 의존), worse than before(이전보다 나쁜)는 회복 맥락과 반대이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to _________________________________________________.
problems.blankTarget: the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda
choices: the generous funding from foreign governments
the strict regulations imposed by local authorities
the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda
the advanced technology used in the counseling sessions
the mandatory participation required by the community leaders
problems.explanation: 원문에서 '당신과 Dr. Chibanda 같은 사람들의 노력 덕분'이라고 명시되어 있다. ①은 외국 정부 자금, ②는 당국 규제, ④는 첨단 기술, ⑤는 의무 참여로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them.
problems.passage: Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. ( ① ) I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. ( ② ) I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. ( ③ ) That's very kind of you. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: 주어진 문장은 '그들을 지지함으로써 상처받은 마음을 치유할 수 있다고 알았다'로, ⑦번 문장의 '무언가 하고 싶었다(wanted to do something about it)'의 구체적 실천 방법을 설명한다. 따라서 ⑦번 문장 바로 뒤인 ③에 들어가야 한다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives.
problems.passage: It's been wonderful. ( ① ) Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. ( ② ) One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. ( ③ ) I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: '사람들의 삶에 변화를 만들 수 있다는 것을 보았다'는 ⑫번 문장의 '도움을 구하는 것이 부끄럽지 않다'를 뒷받침하고, 이어지는 ⑭번 문장의 구체적 사례(19세 소녀)로 자연스럽게 연결되므로 ②가 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 글의 흐름으로 보아, 주어진 문장이 들어가기에 가장 적절한 곳은?
problems.givenSentence: Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program.
problems.passage: One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. ( ① ) Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! ( ② ) It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. ( ③ ) Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. ( ④ )
problems.explanation: '이런 사례들이 프로그램의 일원인 것을 자랑스럽게 만든다'는 소녀의 회복과 사업 시작이라는 성공 사례 직후(②)에 들어가야 한다. 이후 '보람 있는 경험'이라는 일반화로 자연스럽게 이어진다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program?

chunks.A: I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them.
chunks.B: Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo.
chunks.C: I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years.

problems.explanation: (B) 자기 소개(이름) → (C) 나이와 자원봉사 경력 → (A) 참여 동기(지역사회 문제 + 도움 의지). '자기소개 → 경력 → 동기' 순서가 자연스럽다.

problems.instruction: 주어진 글 다음에 이어질 글의 순서로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.givenStart: I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives.

chunks.A: I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business!
chunks.B: Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program.
chunks.C: One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away.

problems.explanation: (C) 구체적 사례 소개(19세 소녀의 어려움) → (A) 상담 과정과 회복 결과 → (B) 'Cases like this'로 사례를 받아 자부심 표현. 'this'가 (A)의 성공 사례를 가리키므로 (A)-(B) 순서가 필수.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program.
choices: Grandma Moyo is a licensed psychiatrist who works at a hospital.
The nineteen-year-old patient struggled because she failed her university exams.
Grandma Moyo joined the program to receive therapy for her own mental health issues.
The young patient's life remained unchanged after the counseling sessions.
Grandma Moyo has been volunteering for the program for about eight years.
problems.explanation: ⑥번 문장 'I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years'와 일치한다. ②는 공인 정신과 의사가 아닌 자원봉사자, ③은 대학 시험 실패가 아닌 어머니 사망, ④는 자신의 치료가 아닌 타인 돕기, ⑤는 변하지 않은 것이 아닌 회복이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하는 것은?
problems.passage: One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business!
choices: The patient's mother encouraged her to start a business before passing away.
Grandma Moyo helped the girl by providing financial assistance for her business.
The patient was a thirty-year-old woman struggling with unemployment.
The girl's recovery journey began before she met Grandma Moyo.
The patient started her own business after her life got back on track.
problems.explanation: ⑯번 문장에서 '삶이 정상 궤도로 돌아오고 자신의 사업까지 시작했다'고 명시되어 있다. ②는 어머니의 권유 언급 없음, ③은 재정 지원 언급 없음, ④는 19세 소녀가 맞음, ⑤는 Grandma Moyo의 상담 후 시작이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business!
choices: Grandma Moyo is 76 years old.
She joined the program to help people with mental health problems.
The girl's father passed away, which caused her a difficult time.
One of her patients was a nineteen-year-old girl.
The girl has started her own business after recovering.
problems.explanation: ⑭번 문장에서 'her mom passed away'라고 명시되어 있으므로, '아버지(father)가 돌아가셨다'는 ④가 일치하지 않는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives.
choices: Grandma Moyo has been a volunteer for about eight years.
She believes seeking help for mental health problems is not shameful.
Grandma Moyo says the volunteer experience has been disappointing and unrewarding.
The patient Grandma Moyo described was a nineteen-year-old girl.
The patient's life improved and she started her own business.
problems.explanation: ⑪번 문장 'It's been wonderful'과 ⑱번 문장 'such a rewarding experience'에서 경험이 훌륭하고 보람차다고 했으므로, '실망스럽고 보람 없다'는 ④가 일치하지 않는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용과 일치하지 않는 것은?
problems.passage: Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: The host thanked Grandma Moyo for sharing her experiences.
The host believes the program has had no meaningful influence whatsoever.
The host finds the program's influence truly inspiring.
The host credits the program's success to people like Grandma Moyo and Dr. Chibanda.
The host expressed gratitude at the end of the interview.
problems.explanation: ⑳번 문장에서 '프로그램이 끼친 영향을 보는 것이 정말 영감을 준다'고 했으므로, '의미 있는 영향이 전혀 없다'는 ④가 일치하지 않는 내용이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives.
problems.summaryTemplate: Grandma Moyo has been volunteering for eight years to help people ____(A)____, and witnessing patients' recovery, such as a girl who started her own business, makes her feel ____(B)____.
choices: (A) with financial difficulties - (B) anxious about the future
(A) with mental health problems - (B) proud and rewarded
(A) find employment opportunities - (B) uncertain about her role
(A) pass their school examinations - (B) frustrated with the system
(A) with mental health problems - (B) exhausted but obligated
problems.explanation: 정신건강 문제(mental health problems)로 어려움을 겪는 사람들을 돕고, 환자 회복을 목격하면서 자부심과 보람(proud and rewarded)을 느낀다. ①은 재정 어려움·미래 불안, ③은 취업·불확실, ④는 시험·좌절, ⑤는 지침·의무로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 내용을 한 문장으로 요약하고자 한다. 빈칸 (A)와 (B)에 들어갈 말로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business!
problems.summaryTemplate: After losing her mother, a young patient received ____(A)____ from Grandma Moyo and eventually ____(B)____.
choices: (A) financial support - (B) moved to another country
(A) medical treatment - (B) returned to the hospital for further care
(A) academic tutoring - (B) graduated from university with honors
(A) counseling - (B) recovered and started her own business
(A) counseling - (B) decided to become a professional therapist herself
problems.explanation: 상담(counseling)을 받아 회복하고 자신의 사업을 시작(recovered and started her own business)했다. ①은 재정 지원·이주, ③은 의료 치료·재입원, ④는 과외·졸업, ⑤는 전문 치료사가 되기로 결심으로 본문과 다르다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 it이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved w<u>it</u>h the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew that I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: the Friendship Bench program's expansion to new regions
Grandma Moyo's personal experience of losing a family member
the government's policy on public health services
the financial support provided by international donors
seeking help for mental health problems
problems.explanation: it은 앞 문장의 주어 'Seeking help for mental health problems'를 가리킨다. 정신건강 문제에 대해 도움을 구하는 것이 사람들의 삶에 변화를 만들 수 있다는 문맥이다.

problems.instruction: 밑줄 친 that이 가리키는 것으로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.passage: That's really amazing! Thank you for your time, Dr. Chibanda. Now let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program. Can you introduce yourself and tell us how you got involved with the Friendship Bench program? Hello, my name is Judith Moyo, but people call me Grandma Moyo. I'm 76 years old, and I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years. I joined the program because many people in my community struggle with mental health problems, and I wanted to do something about it. I knew <u>that</u> I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting them. That's very kind of you. Can you tell us about your experience as a grandmother for the Friendship Bench program? It's been wonderful. Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed of. I've seen how it can make a difference in people's lives. One patient of mine, a nineteen-year-old girl, was going through a difficult time after her mom passed away. I counseled her and helped her begin her recovery journey. Now her life is back on track, and she has even started her own business! Cases like this make me so proud to be part of the program. It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes in their lives. Thanks for sharing your experiences with us today, Grandma Moyo. It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program has had. And it's all thanks to the hard work of people like you and Dr. Chibanda.
choices: a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'that thing'
a conjunction introducing a noun clause
an adverb modifying the verb 'see'
a relative pronoun referring to 'the influence'
a conjunction introducing a reason clause
problems.explanation: that은 선행사 'the influence'를 수식하는 목적격 관계대명사이다. 'the Friendship Bench program has had'에서 had의 목적어 역할을 한다. ②는 지시대명사, ③은 명사절 접속사, ④는 부사, ⑤는 이유절 접속사로 쓰임이 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Now let's ①<u>hear</u> from one of the grandmothers who ②<u>volunteers</u> for the program. People ③<u>call</u> me Grandma Moyo. I have been ④<u>volunteered</u> for the program for about eight years. Seeking help for mental health problems is ⑤<u>nothing</u> to be ashamed of.
choices: ①
②
③
⑤
④
problems.explanation: have been + V-ing(현재완료진행)이므로 volunteered → volunteering. ① let's + V원형 ✓ ② 주격관계대명사 who + 3인칭 단수 volunteers ✓ ③ call + O + OC(5형식) ✓ ⑤ nothing to be ashamed of ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: Can you tell us how you ①<u>got involved</u> with the program? I knew that I could help ②<u>heal</u> their wounded hearts by ③<u>supporting</u> them. Seeking help is nothing to ④<u>be ashamed</u> of. I counseled her and helped her ⑤<u>to beginning</u> her recovery journey.
choices: ①
②
③
⑤
④
problems.explanation: help + O + V원형 / to V이므로 to beginning → begin (또는 to begin). ① get involved with ✓ ② help + V원형 ✓ ③ by + V-ing ✓ ④ nothing to be ashamed of ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 글의 밑줄 친 부분 중, 어법상 틀린 것은?
problems.passage: One patient of mine was ①<u>going through</u> a difficult time after her mom ②<u>passed away</u>. Now her life is back on track, and she ③<u>has started</u> her own business! Cases like this ④<u>make</u> me so proud. It's truly inspiring ⑤<u>seeing</u> the influence that the program has had.
choices: ①
②
⑤
③
④
problems.explanation: It is + 형용사 + to V (가주어 구문)이므로 seeing → to see. ① was going through(과거진행) ✓ ② passed away(과거) ✓ ③ has started(현재완료) ✓ ④ make + O + 형용사(5형식) ✓

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 to부정사의 용법이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: Seeking help is nothing <u>to be ashamed</u> of.
It is such a rewarding experience <u>to help</u> people.
It's truly inspiring <u>to see</u> the influence the program has had.
Cases like this make me so proud <u>to be</u> part of the program.
I helped her <u>begin</u> her recovery journey.
problems.explanation: ①은 형용사적 용법(nothing을 수식), ②③은 명사적 용법(가주어 it의 진주어), ④는 부사적 용법(감정의 원인), ⑤는 help 뒤 원형부정사. ①만 형용사적 용법으로 다르다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 it(It)의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: I've seen how <u>it</u> can make a difference in people's lives.
<u>It</u> is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes.
<u>It</u>'s truly inspiring to see the influence the program has had.
<u>It</u> is important to exercise regularly.
<u>It</u> was difficult to find a solution to the problem.
problems.explanation: ③의 it은 앞 문장의 'seeking help for mental health problems'를 받는 대명사이고, 나머지 ①②④⑤는 모두 가주어 it(진주어 to부정사를 대신)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 who의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: one of the grandmothers <u>who</u> volunteers for the program
<u>Who</u> told you about the program?
the girl <u>who</u> started her own business
people <u>who</u> struggle with mental health problems
the doctor <u>who</u> founded the Friendship Bench
problems.explanation: ④의 Who는 의문대명사('누가'), 나머지 ①②③⑤는 모두 주격 관계대명사(선행사를 수식)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 중 밑줄 친 make의 쓰임이 나머지 넷과 다른 것은?
choices: Cases like this <u>make</u> me so proud.
The news <u>made</u> her happy.
The cold weather <u>made</u> him sleepy.
help people <u>make</u> positive changes in their lives
The movie <u>made</u> the audience emotional.
problems.explanation: ③의 make는 '만들다/이루다'(make changes = 변화를 만들다)로 일반 타동사이고, 나머지 ①②④⑤는 모두 make + O + 형용사(5형식 사역/결과)이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Now let's hearing from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program.
problems.explanation: let's + V원형 (청유문). let 뒤에는 원형부정사가 온다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: My name is Judith Moyo, but people call me as Grandma Moyo.
problems.explanation: call + O + OC (5형식). call은 목적어와 목적격 보어 사이에 전치사 as를 쓰지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I have been volunteered for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years.
problems.explanation: have been + V-ing (현재완료진행). '8년간 계속 자원봉사해 오고 있다'는 진행의 의미이므로 volunteering이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be ashamed.
problems.explanation: be ashamed of = '~을 부끄러워하다'. ashamed는 전치사 of와 함께 쓰인다. nothing to be ashamed of = '부끄러워할 것이 없는'에서 전치사 of가 문미에 잔류한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: I counseled her and helped her beginning her recovery journey.
problems.explanation: help + O + V원형 / to V. help는 동명사(beginning)를 목적격 보어로 취하지 않는다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: It is such a rewarding experience helping people make positive changes in their lives.
problems.explanation: It is + 명사구 + to V (가주어 구문). It이 가주어이고 to help가 진주어이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 문장에서 어법상 틀린 부분을 찾아 바르게 고치시오.
problems.stem: It's truly inspiring to see the influence which the Friendship Bench program has.
problems.explanation: 프로그램이 '끼쳐 온' 영향이므로 현재완료 has had가 적절하다. 과거부터 현재까지의 영향을 나타내는 현재완료 경험/계속 용법.

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: let's hear from one of the grandmothers who volunteers
problems.explanation: let's + V원형(hear from) + 목적어(one of the grandmothers) + 관계대명사절(who volunteers)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.stem: I'm 76 years old, and _________________________________ for about eight years.
problems.answer: I have been volunteering for the Friendship Bench program
problems.explanation: have been + V-ing(현재완료진행) + for the Friendship Bench program(전치사구)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: I could help heal their wounded hearts by supporting
problems.explanation: I could(주어+조동사) + help heal(help + V원형) + their wounded hearts(목적어) + by supporting(수단 전치사구)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: Cases like this make me so proud to be part
problems.explanation: Cases like this(주어) + make me so proud(make + O + 형용사) + to be part of(감정의 원인 to부정사)

problems.instruction: 주어진 단어를 올바른 순서로 배열하여 문장을 완성하시오.
problems.answer: It is such a rewarding experience to help people make positive changes
problems.explanation: It is(가주어) + such a rewarding experience(보어) + to help people(진주어) + make positive changes(help + O + V원형)

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Now let's _______ (hear) from one of the grandmothers who volunteers for the program.
choices: hear
hearing
to hear
heard
to hearing
problems.explanation: let's + V원형. let은 사역동사로 원형부정사를 취한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I have been _______ (volunteer) for the Friendship Bench program for about eight years.
choices: volunteered
volunteering
volunteer
to volunteer
being volunteered
problems.explanation: have been + V-ing (현재완료진행). 8년간 계속 봉사해 오고 있다는 진행의 의미.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I knew that I could help _______ (heal) their wounded hearts by supporting them.
choices: healed
healing
to healing
having healed
heal
problems.explanation: help + V원형 / to V. heal(원형부정사)이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Seeking help for mental health problems is nothing to be _______ (ashame) of.
choices: ashaming
ashamed
ashame
to ashame
being ashamed
problems.explanation: be ashamed of = '~을 부끄러워하다'. nothing to be ashamed of에서 ashamed(형용사)가 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: I counseled her and helped her _______ (begin) her recovery journey.
choices: beginning
to beginning
begun
begin
having begun
problems.explanation: help + O + V원형 / to V. begin(원형부정사)이 적절하다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: Now her life is back on track, and she _______ (start) her own business!
choices: even starts
is even starting
even started
had even started
has even started
problems.explanation: 현재완료 has started(결과 용법): 사업을 시작한 결과가 현재까지 유효. even이 포함된 has even started가 원문과 일치한다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: It is such a rewarding experience _______ (help) people make positive changes in their lives.
choices: help
helping
to help
helped
to helping
problems.explanation: It is + 명사구 + to V (가주어 구문). It이 가주어이고 to help가 진주어이다.

problems.instruction: 다음 빈칸에 들어갈 말의 형태로 가장 적절한 것은?
problems.stem: It's truly inspiring to see the influence that the Friendship Bench program _______ (have).
choices: has
had
has had
having
is having
problems.explanation: 프로그램이 '끼쳐 온' 영향이므로 현재완료 has had가 적절하다. 과거부터 현재까지 지속된 영향을 나타낸다.
